journey.name: Settings star toggle
journey.description: The header gear opens a full-screen two-column all-apps list. Unstarring an app dims its row and removes it from home; re-starring restores it. Toggles persist across a full app restart.

actions.action: Launch the Nocturne launcher (com.pavlovsfrog.minimaltvlauncher) and verify the app grid is shown and a gear (Settings) button is present in the top-left of the header.
Move focus to the gear button and press OK.

Verify a full-screen settings surface appears with a "‹ Back" pill, an "Apps" title, a subtitle of the form "{n} of {m} on home · press to star or unstar", and a two-column list of all installed apps where visible apps show a filled star and hidden apps show a hollow star with a dimmed name.
Note the name of the first row's app and its subtitle count, then press OK on the row.
Verify the row's star flips to hollow, its name dims, the subtitle count drops by one, and focus stays on the row.
Press Back and verify home is shown again with that app no longer on the grid.
Open settings again via the gear, press OK on the same row to re-star it, and verify the star fills and the subtitle count rises by one.
Close settings and verify the app is back on the home grid.
Force-stop and relaunch the launcher; verify the grid still shows the re-starred app.
